name: Self-Correcting Code Generation
description: Generate code, run it in a sandbox, branch on the exit code, and feed the error back to a debugger LLM that fixes and re-runs — a closed loop grounded in real execution output.

data.label: Generate → run → fix
data.text: The Generator drafts code; the Code Executor runs it. The Condition branches on the exit_code: 0 returns the result, anything else routes to the Debugger which reads the stderr, fixes the code, and re-runs it. MAX_NODE_VISITS bounds the loop.

data.label: Generator
data.systemPrompt: Write Python code that satisfies the spec. Output ONLY the code, no prose.

data.label: Debugger
data.systemPrompt: Read the stderr above. Diagnose the failure and rewrite the code to fix it. Output ONLY the corrected code.

data.label: The fix loop
data.text: First Code Executor run is simulated as a NameError. The Condition's first branch matches when 'exit_code": 0' appears in the output (a passing run); otherwise it loops to the Debugger and re-executes.
